info.name: Universal Store APIs
info.description:
Contract and integration tests for the **Catalog** and **Inventory** microservices in the Universal Store take-home assessment.

## Purpose

This collection verifies that both services:

- Return correct HTTP status codes
    
- Match expected response shapes
    
- Serve seeded test data correctly
    
- Honor the cross-service contract: every inventory SKU must exist in the catalog
    

It is intended for **interactive debugging in Postman** and **automated runs via Newman** (`tests/postman/`).

## Prerequisites

- **Catalog service** running at `{{catalogUrl}}` (default: `http://localhost:3000`)
    
- **Inventory service** running at `{{inventoryUrl}}` (default: `http://localhost:4000`)
    
- Databases seeded (via `docker compose up` or manual seed scripts)
    

Select the **Local** environment before running requests.

## Collection structure

### Catalog

| Request | Validates |
| --- | --- |
| `GET /catalog` | 200, array of ≥4 items with `sku`, `title`, `price`, `image`; stores SKUs in `catalogSkus` |
| `GET /catalog/:sku` (exists) | 200, returns Pen (`111111`) |
| `GET /catalog/:sku` (404) | 404 for unknown SKU |

### Inventory

| Request | Validates |
| --- | --- |
| `GET /inventory` | 200, array of ≥4 items with `sku` and `quantity`; SKU `444444` has quantity `0` |
| `GET /inventory/:sku` | 200, returns array; SKU `111111` has quantity `3` |

### Contract

| Request | Validates |
| --- | --- |
| Every inventory SKU exists in catalog | Each inventory SKU is present in `catalogSkus` (set by `GET /catalog`) |

## Run order

Run **Catalog → Inventory → Contract** when using Collection Runner. The Contract test depends on `catalogSkus`, which is set by the first Catalog request.

## Running via Newman

\`\`\`bash  
cd tests/postman  
npm run test:postman

item.name: Catalog
item.description:
Tests for the **Catalog** microservice (`{{catalogUrl}}`, port 3000).

Returns product metadata: `sku`, `title`, `price`, and `image`.

| Request | Validates |
| --- | --- |
| `GET /catalog` | 200, array of ≥4 items; stores SKUs in `catalogSkus` |
| `GET /catalog/:sku` (exists) | 200, returns Pen (`111111`) |
| `GET /catalog/:sku` (404) | 404 for unknown SKU |

item.name: Inventory

exec: pm.test('444444 is out of stock in seed data', () => pm.expect(marker.quantity).to.eql(0));

item.description:
Tests for the **Inventory** microservice (`{{inventoryUrl}}`, port 4000).

Returns stock levels keyed by `sku` and `quantity`.

| Request | Validates |
| --- | --- |
| `GET /inventory` | 200, array of ≥4 items; SKU `444444` has quantity `0` |
| `GET /inventory/:sku` | 200, returns array; SKU `111111` has quantity `3` |

item.name: Contract
Every inventory SKU exists in catalog

item.description:
Cross-service contract tests between Catalog and Inventory.

The **Product Landing Page (PLP)** aggregates data from both services to show in-stock products. These tests verify the underlying data contract PLP depends on: every inventory SKU must exist in the catalog.

| Request | Validates |
| --- | --- |
| Every inventory SKU exists in catalog | Each inventory SKU is in `catalogSkus` (set by Catalog `GET /catalog`) |

**Must run after the Catalog folder** so `catalogSkus` is populated.
